analysis: MFJ 2024: AGI $208K, TI $178.8K, total tax $26,832. Effective tax rate (Line 24 / Line 9) = 12.5%. Marginal bracket ~ 22%. Schedule C accounting (SSTB) with $215K net profit. Couple TI BEFORE QBI = $208K - $29.2K = $178.8K. MFJ §199A threshold floor for 2024 is $383,900 - couple is well BELOW threshold, so SSTB classification of accounting is irrelevant. The preparer missed the QBI deduction. With QBI: 20% × $215,000 = $43,000 deduction; corrected TI = $135,800; corrected tax ~ $19K instead of $26,832. Estimated refund of $7,800 from amended return.
conclusion: Return summary: MFJ 2024, AGI $208K, current TI $178.8K, current total tax $26,832 (effective 12.5%). FLAG #1: §199A QBI deduction omitted. Couple is below MFJ threshold ($383,900); SSTB / W-2 / UBIA limits inapplicable; full 20% deduction available on $215K Schedule C net profit. Recommend amended return (Form 1040-X) to claim ~ $43K QBI deduction; estimated refund ~ $7,800. FLAG #2: $4K Schedule B interest may trigger NIIT if AGI exceeds threshold (MFJ $250K NIIT floor — AGI $208K is below, so no NIIT). FLAG #3: Self-employment tax already on Schedule SE (route to confirm). FLAG #4: §6654 estimated-tax safe-harbor for 2025 (route to `due-date-calculator`). NO tax-position advice; routes to `tax-research-federal` (for §199A position) and `predict-qbi-eligibility` (for QBI computation).
band_rationale: Extraction-confidence high (user provided structured input). NOT tax-position confidence (which is outside this skill's scope).

authorities.quoted_text: Line 13 reports Qualified Business Income deduction.
